text.title: When to skip plan.md entirely
text.summary: Not every feature needs plan.md. Trivial features ride on the spec.md task checklist alone.

rule.statement: SKIP plan.md when the feature has ≤3 tasks AND introduces no new contract.
rule.rationale: For tiny features the plan.md overhead exceeds the value. The spec.md task list is structure enough.

example.note: Feature: "add `phone` field to user profile form". 1 migration + 1 form change + 1 test. Spec.md lists the three tasks as checkboxes. No plan.md.

Feature: "add Stripe webhook handler" — 6 tasks, new signature-verification contract, idempotency requirement. Skipping plan.md leaves the dependency graph unwritten and parallel execution impossible.

rule.statement: If you cannot answer "is this trivial?" in one sentence, the answer is no — write plan.md.
rule.rationale: The bias should tilt toward writing the plan. The cost of one extra plan.md is low; the cost of an unplanned mid-size feature is high.
